title: How I Cut Requirements Analysis from Weeks to Hours with AI Agents
hook: The first time I used Codex agents, our requirements analysis dropped from weeks to just hours.
partner_takeaway: Readers learn a step-by-step workflow to leverage AI agents for drastically faster requirements analysis in software projects.
reasoning: Combining a personal shipping story with a high-relevance industry case lets readers see exactly how to implement AI agents. This is timely as many are searching for practical ways to speed up delivery and cut bottlenecks.

title: Why AI’s ‘1997 Internet Moment’ Matters for Your Job
hook: AI’s current phase feels eerily similar to the internet in 1997: everyone is guessing, but few see where value will concentrate.
partner_takeaway: Readers gain a nuanced perspective on how AI’s misunderstood job impacts and emerging value areas mirror historic tech shifts, helping them anticipate real changes.
reasoning: This idea links the writer’s personal shift in perspective to a widely discussed trend, giving readers actionable context for market changes. It’s relevant as AI headlines are confusing and readers need clarity on what actually matters.

title: A Pattern for Using AI Tools That Actually Works
hook: Every time I fall back on this workflow, I get more done—AI isn’t magic, but it’s a multiplier if you use it right.
partner_takeaway: Readers learn a proven pattern for integrating AI tools into their daily work, including when it works best and common pitfalls.
reasoning: This is a tactical, immediately useful post for the Practical AI track. It draws from lived experience, so readers get a real-world workflow they can try this week.

title: Why $1B Exits Are Dead—and What It Means for Builders
hook: Venture capital is shifting: AI accelerates startup growth, but billion-dollar exits are getting rare.
partner_takeaway: Readers learn why AI changes exit dynamics in tech, making larger outcomes possible but traditional $1B exits less common.
reasoning: This gives industry readers a sharp summary of a major trend, with a clear takeaway they can use to rethink their own scaling and investment strategies.

title: Shipping X: What I Learned from My First Production Launch
hook: I still remember the nerves and excitement the first time I shipped X to production—here’s what actually happened.
partner_takeaway: Readers see what really happens during a first production launch, including unexpected lessons and tactical advice for their own projects.
reasoning: This is a classic ‘building in public’ post, showing concrete lessons from a personal milestone. It’s valuable for readers who want real talk about shipping and learning.
